samples.xml_description:
<module id="synchronizer">
    <description>Implement a multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk_a">Clock signal A.</input>
        <input id="clk_b">Clock signal B.</input>
        <input id="arstn">Active-low reset signal A.</input>
        <input id="brstn">Active-low reset signal B.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[3:0]">Input data signal.</input>
        <input id="data_en">Input enable signal.</input>
        <output id="dataout" width_description="[3:0]">Output data signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="data_reg">Register to store input data.</parameter>
        <parameter id="en_data_reg">Register to store input enable signal.</parameter>
        <parameter id="en_clap_one">Control register for selection of input data.</parameter>
        <parameter id="en_clap_two">Control register for selection of input data.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="data_reg">Register to store input data.</sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="en_data_reg">Register to store input enable signal.</sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="en_clap_one">Control register for selection of input data.</sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="en_clap_two">Control register for selection of input data.</sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module includes a register, data_reg, to store the input data signal, data_in. 
    On the positive edge of clock signal A (clk_a) or the falling edge of reset signal A (arstn), the module updates the register. 
    If the reset signal A (arstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, the register (data_reg) is set to 0. 
    If the reset signal A (arstn) is high, the register (data_reg) is updated with the input data signal (data_in).
    </implementation>
    <task>
    The module includes a register, en_data_reg, to store the input enable signal, data_en. 
    On the positive edge of clock signal A (clk_a) or the falling edge of reset signal A (arstn), the module updates the register. 
    If the reset signal A (arstn) is low, the register (en_data_reg) is set to 0. 
    If the reset signal A (arstn) is high, the register (en_data_reg) is updated with the input enable signal (data_en).
    </task>
    <task>
    The module includes two registers, en_clap_one and en_clap_two, to control the selection of the input data. 
    On the positive edge of clock signal B (clk_b) or the falling edge of reset signal B (brstn), the module updates the registers. 
    If the reset signal B (brstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, both registers (en_clap_one and en_clap_two) are set to 0.
    If the reset signal B (brstn) is high, the registers (en_clap_one and en_clap_two) are updated based on the value of en_data_reg. 
    The register en_clap_one is assigned the value of en_data_reg, and en_clap_two is assigned the previous value of en_clap_one.
    </task>
    <task>
    On the positive edge of clock signal B (clk_b) or the falling edge of reset signal B (brstn), the module assigns the output data value. 
    If the reset signal B (brstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, the output data (dataout) is set to 0. 
    If the reset signal B (brstn) is high and the control signal (en_clap_two) is active, the output data (dataout) is assigned the value of the data register (data_reg). 
    If the control signal (en_clap_two) is inactive, the output data (dataout) retains its previous value.
    </task>
</module>
